mission.title: confidential-containers: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers
mission.description: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current confidential-containers deployment
steps.description:
Verify your confidential-containers version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers
helm list -n confidential-containers 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working confidential-containers installation.

steps.title: Review confidential-containers configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant confidential-containers configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers
kubectl get configmap -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=confidential-containers
```
Currently, we are facing some typical problems in CoCo.

1. How to configure KBS certificates every launch of TEE without embed certs inside guest images? This is not restricted to only KBS certs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers
steps.description:
> Enlightening guest components to query the OPA engine seems a bit clunky to me. That will be more complex and slower (performance probably not significant) than just reading a config file. 

This is in the same category as enlightening the guest components to support attestation or fetching
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
name: pod1
annotations:
  io.katacontainers.config.runtime.cc_init_data: 
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers
kubectl get events -n confidential-containers --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> Enlightening guest components to query the OPA engine seems a bit clunky to me. That will be more complex and slower (performance probably not significant) than just reading a config file. 

This is in the same category as enlightening the guest components to support attestation or fetching things from KBS.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
name: pod1
annotations:
  io.katacontainers.config.runtime.cc_init_data: 

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with confidential-containers installed or the issue environment reproducible.
